q: What is the principle behind vaccination?
options: Vaccines introduce fully virulent pathogens so the body can practice eliminating them in a controlled setting
Vaccines provide preformed antibodies from another organism so the host doesn't need to make its own
Vaccines expose the immune system to antigens (from killed/weakened pathogens or their components) to stimulate a primary immune response and memory cell formation without causing disease
Vaccines permanently alter host DNA to encode resistance genes against specific pathogens
explanation: Vaccines present antigens in a safe form; the adaptive immune system mounts a primary response and retains memory B and T cells, enabling a rapid secondary response upon real exposure.
